summary: Iter 4 — Backend 22/22 PASS (added 4 tests: /auth/me bio+picture keys, profile bio+picture persistence via PUT->GET, picture > 1.6M chars returns 413, login with seeded test@example.com returns bio+picture). All prior endpoints (chat, find-help, dashboard, settings, requests) still green. Frontend: Profile fully working — file picker uploads tiny PNG, FileReader -> data URL, profile-avatar-img reflects after save (data:image/png prefix), bio text persists across reload, navbar profile-chip img appears after upload, Remove photo (profile-avatar-remove) clears the avatar and saves. Email field is disabled. All 6 cat-* testids (cat-food/medical/financial/shelter/mental/education) present and clickable. Find Help HIGH urgency: 6 cards each with UNIQUE image src (6/6 unique pexels URLs), urgency-badge color rgb(220,38,38)=#DC2626 ✓, all 6 cards show 'URGENT' pill with white text on red bg ✓. Chat page renders warm gradient header with image and HeartHandshake bot avatar (visually confirmed via screenshot, though no testid). Floating AI button visible.

issues: Spec listed section data-testids hero/quick-actions/support-categories/ai-chat-showcase/how-it-works/impact/final-cta but ONLY request-help-section exists in code. Sections render visually fine (8-section flow verified iter 3) but section-level testids are missing for stable structural assertions.

Header (warm gradient + image) and bot avatar (HeartHandshake icon) render correctly visually but no data-testid attributes exist for chat-header or chat-bot-avatar — recommended to add testids for header/avatar so future tests can assert image presence directly instead of computed-style sniffing.

action_items: [OPTIONAL/LOW] Add section-level data-testids on Home.jsx (hero, quick-actions, support-categories, ai-chat-showcase, how-it-works, impact, final-cta) to match the original spec naming.

[OPTIONAL/LOW] Add data-testid='chat-header' on the warm gradient header div in Chat.jsx and data-testid='chat-bot-avatar' on the HeartHandshake icon container so the warm header image and friendly avatar can be asserted in automated tests.
critical_code_review_comments: Profile.jsx: 'Remove photo' button is correctly conditionally rendered behind {form.picture && ...} — earlier ambiguity in test was just stale state from prior runs (test user already had a saved picture). No code change needed.
Backend size guard at server.py:319 uses 1_600_000 chars cutoff which corresponds to ~1.2MB raw — matches the spec exactly. Returning 413 with proper detail is correct.
Find Help imageFor() rotation by index in FindHelp.jsx works — 6 cards each got 6 distinct pexels URLs from the IMAGE_POOLS map for the same Food category.
Urgency badge and per-card URGENT pill both correctly use #DC2626 (rgb(220,38,38)) for HIGH urgency — visually confirmed via getComputedStyle.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all spec-listed flows green; only minor missing testids on Home sections + Chat header)

test_credentials: test@example.com / Pass1234! (seeded). New TEST_ users created via signup with timestamped emails for backend tests.
seed_data_creation: 1 new TEST user per backend run (signup+<ts>@example.com); seeded test@example.com used for all frontend flows. Test user picture/bio updated and then cleared during the run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests (22) cover signup/login/me/google-session/profile (incl bio+picture persistence + 413), requests CRUD, chat (LLM normal + emergency + history + empty), find-help, dashboard, settings. Frontend: Profile picture upload+save+persist+remove pipeline is end-to-end working with base64 data URLs. Find Help HIGH urgency renders red urgency-badge + URGENT red pills + 6 unique images. Cat-* testids all present. Spec-listed Home section testids and Chat header/avatar testids are missing — sections themselves render fine, just no testids. Auth-guard, signup-success overlay, FAB all still working.
rca of the issue: No issues found. The two minor 'missing testid' callouts are spec/code mismatches, not bugs — the underlying UI features (8 home sections, warm chat header with image, HeartHandshake bot avatar) all render correctly per visual inspection.
